## Promotion Gate

- [ ] Finding was classified as `Promote to parent` or `Escalate`.
- [ ] Existing open and recently closed refinement issues were checked.
- [ ] Weekly parent issue capacity was checked or an escalation reason is listed.
- [ ] Evidence is strong enough to justify durable work.

## Recommended Slice

Describe the first safe, reviewable slice. If this is larger than one MR, create
or link sub-issues before implementation starts.

## Definition Of Ready

- [ ] Problem is clear.
- [ ] Target product surface is clear.
- [ ] Duplicate check is complete.
- [ ] First slice is identified.
- [ ] MR-sized child issues are created or linked before implementation starts.
- [ ] Parent Codex coordinator thread is recorded above.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The improvement objective is clear.
- [ ] Duplicate candidates have been checked.
- [ ] MR-sized sub-issues are created or linked.
- [ ] The parent Codex coordinator thread is recorded above.
- [ ] Child issue threads are spawned from the parent thread.

## Outcome Summary

- Did this initiative improve the product?
- What should other teams reuse?
- What follow-up work remains?

## Codex Thread Protocol

- Reuse the Codex coordinator thread recorded in `codex-thread-map`.
- Spawn child issue threads from this parent thread after each child issue exists.
- Synthesize child outcomes back into this issue before closing it.
